schema_name: Synthetic Conversation Turn Schema (Moshi Training Data)
description: Field-by-field reference for each key in a conversation turn JSONL record, limited strictly to the fields listed on the whiteboard.

turn_id.description: Unique identifier for this turn within a single conversation. Used so other turns can reference it (e.g. via overlaps_with, interrupted_by).
turn_id.possible_values: t1, t2, t3, ... tN (sequential, unique per conversation)

speaker.description: Which of the two fixed speaker slots produced this turn.

text.description: The actual spoken content of the turn, generated by the writer LLM.

emotion.description: Emotion tag for this specific turn.

planned_start_sec.description: Estimated start time of the turn, generated by the LLM at script stage before any audio is rendered.

planned_end_sec.description: Estimated end time of the turn, generated by the LLM at the same script stage as planned_start_sec.

real_start_sec.description: Actual start time of the turn on the rendered audio, obtained via WhisperX (forced alignment) or manual calculation.
real_start_sec.possible_values: Numeric float from WhisperX / manual calculation

real_end_sec.description: Actual end time of the turn on the rendered audio, obtained via WhisperX (forced alignment) or manual calculation.
real_end_sec.possible_values: Numeric float from WhisperX / manual calculation, greater than real_start_sec

error_time.description: Difference between planned timing and real timing for this turn.
error_time.possible_values: planned_duration minus real_duration (or real minus planned); negative/positive/zero float

turn_type.description: Classifies the nature of this turn in the conversation's turn-taking structure.

overlaps_with.description: The turn_id of the other turn that this turn overlaps in time with.
overlaps_with.possible_values: Any valid turn_id from the same conversation

overlaps_kind.description: Describes the relationship type of the overlap referenced by overlaps_with.

interrupted.description: Whether this turn was cut off by another (interrupting) turn before it naturally finished.

interrupted_by.description: The turn_id of the turn that interrupted/cut off this turn.
interrupted_by.possible_values: Any valid turn_id from the same conversation
